articles.title: CESTAT allows Amendment of Shipping Bills Due to Absence of Time Limit During Transition Period
articles.summary: The CESTAT ruled that time limits introduced later cannot be applied retrospectively to deny amendment requests for shipping bills during a transition period. This allows exporters to correct past errors when schemes change. The decision clarifies that exporters can seek amendments for past mistakes without being restricted by new time limits. The ruling applies to customs and trade-related processes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that time limits introduced later cannot apply retrospectively to deny amendment requests. The ruling clarifies that exporters can seek corrections for past errors during scheme transitions. The post CESTAT allows Amendment of Shipping Bills Due to Absence of Time Limit During Transition Period appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST Cancellation Set Aside as SCN Lacked name or designation of proper officer
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside the cancellation of a taxpayer's GST registration because the show-cause notice (SCN) failed to specify the name or designation of the proper officer. The Court held that this defect violated principles of natural justice. The ruling underscores the importance of procedural compliance in tax administration and could influence future GST enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Court set aside cancellation of GST registration as the notice failed to specify the proper officer and lacked legal validity. It held that such defects violate principles of natural justice. The post GST Cancellation Set Aside as SCN Lacked name or designation of proper officer appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Capital Gains Taxability under Income-tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The article explains when capital gains become taxable under India's Income-tax Act, 1961. It clarifies that gains are generally taxed in the year of transfer, with exceptions for cases like compensation or conversions. The timing of asset transfer is critical for determining tax liability. The analysis is based on existing legal provisions under the Act.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns when capital gains become taxable under the law. The framework clarifies that gains are taxed in the year of transfer, with exceptions for specific cases like compensation and conversions. articles.title: Lok Sabha passes Jan Vishwas amendment bill
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha has approved the Jan Vishwas Bill. This legislation aims to simplify business and daily life by decriminalising minor offences. It will amend 784 provisions across 79 Central Acts. The bill seeks to remove outdated rules and rationalise over 1,000 offences. This move is expected to benefit individuals and MSMEs by improving the regulatory environment.

This move is expected to benefit individuals and MSMEs by improving the regulatory environment.

articles.title: Changes to trans rights act will worsen access to healthcare for vulnerable individuals
articles.summary: The article reports that proposed changes to India's trans rights act could worsen healthcare access for vulnerable individuals by giving government and medical authorities more control over identity determination. This may discourage some groups from relying on institutions that are already discriminatory. The changes could have significant implications for transgender rights and healthcare equity.

articles.title: New I-T Act marks new chapter in India's tax administration, step towards Viksit Bharat: CBDT
articles.summary: India has a new Income Tax Act, 2025, effective April 1, 2026. This replaces the old 1961 Act, making tax rules simpler and easier to follow. articles.title: Government exempts critical petrochemical products from customs duty amid West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The Indian government has exempted critical petrochemical products from customs duty until June 30 to address supply chain disruptions amid the West Asia crisis. This move benefits industries reliant on petrochemical feedstock, including plastics, textiles, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and automotive components. The exemption aims to stabilize manufacturing sectors dependent on these imports. The decision was announced by the government and applies nationwide.

articles.title: GST Registration Restoration to Be Considered on Filing Returns & Payment of Dues: Gauhati HC

The decision highlights that compliance can lead to reconsideration of cancellation. This impacts businesses facing GST registration issues due to non-filing or payment delays.

articles.title: Judicial officers will decide 60L Bengal claims by Apr 7, 47.3L completed: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed that all deleted voter claims in West Bengal must be decided by April 7, ahead of the assembly elections. Judicial officers have been tasked with ensuring independence from political interference. Appellate tribunals have been formed to hear objections. Further hearings are scheduled for April 6.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has been informed that all deleted voter claims in West Bengal will be decided by April 7. This addresses concerns about the upcoming assembly elections. Appellate tribunals have been formed to hear any objections. The court emphasized that judicial officers must remain independent and free from political interference. Further hearings are scheduled for April 6.

articles.title: Centre grants full customs duty exemption on critical petrochemical products amid Middle East crisis: Check list
articles.summary: The Indian government has announced a full exemption on customs duties for critical petrochemical products until June 30, amid disruptions in Middle East energy supplies. This move aims to ease supply chain pressures, reduce costs for downstream industries, and ensure the availability of essential inputs for manufacturing. The exemption applies to key petrochemical items, as notified by the government.
articles.raw_summary: Amidst ongoing Middle East energy disruption, the government has announced a complete customs duty exemption on critical petrochemical products until June 30. This temporary measure aims to alleviate supply chain challenges, reduce cost pressures on downstream industries, and ensure the continued availability of essential inputs for various manufacturing sectors.

The bill aims to counter recent regulatory changes affecting land leases in the region.

articles.title: Bank stocks fall up to 4% as RBI's forex rules may deliver Rs 5,000 crore shock
articles.summary: Indian bank stocks fell up to 4% as the Reserve Bank of India's aggressive currency defense measures threatened to cause losses of up to Rs 5,000 crore. These stringent forex curbs, including barring rupee non-deliverable forwards, sent the rupee surging but hit lenders by closing loopholes used to mitigate earlier restrictions.

A forensic audit revealed alleged misuse of funds, including siphoning through subsidiaries and fictitious transactions, causing a Rs 3,750 crore loss.

articles.title: 12% Profit Estimation & Rejection of Books Set Aside Due to Lack of Justification
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] failed to provide adequate reasons for rejecting a taxpayer's books of accounts under Section 145(3) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal set aside the rejection and remanded the matter for fresh examination. This decision highlights the importance of proper justification in tax assessments and could impact future tax administration practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that the CIT(A) failed to provide reasons for rejecting books under Section 145(3). It remanded the matter for fresh examination of this issue. The post 12% Profit Estimation   Rejection of Books Set Aside Due to Lack of Justification appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: M.P. SLSA Flags Off 51 'Nyay Sarthi' Mobile Legal Aid Clinic Vehicles Across Madhya Pradesh

The vehicles were flagged off by Justice Sanjeev Sachdeva, Chief Justice of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and Patron-in-Chief of MPSLSA, in the presence of Justice Vivek Rusia, Administrative Judge and Executive Chairman, MPSLSA, along with Companion Judges of the High Court.

articles.title: Telangana relied on RBI borrowings for most of FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: Telangana's finances in 2024-25 saw extensive use of RBI's Special Drawing Facility and Ways and Means Advances. The state government borrowed Rs 27,730 crore via SDF and Rs 64,188 crore through WMA. Overdrafts totaling Rs 37,457 crore were also availed. This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

articles.title: Global market rattled as Trump signals longer Iran conflict
articles.summary: Global markets reacted negatively after U.S. President Trump suggested the conflict with Iran could last longer. Investor sentiment dampened, with equity markets falling and safe-haven demand boosting the U.S. dollar. Oil prices rose due to concerns over supply disruptions. The uncertainty around the conflict's duration is now a key focus for investors.
articles.raw_summary: Global markets are cautious. U.S. President Trump indicated the conflict with Iran may last longer. This news has dampened investor sentiment. Equity markets reacted negatively. Safe-haven demand supported the U.S. dollar. Oil prices moved higher due to supply disruption concerns. Investors are now focused on the uncertainty surrounding the conflict's duration.

articles.title: From assembly to ambition: How India is building its electronics backbone
articles.summary: India is shifting from electronics assembly to full-scale manufacturing as the ECMS scheme boosts domestic component production. articles.title: 5 doctors arrested after ₹50,000 payment dispute exposes large kidney racket in Kanpur
articles.summary: A student’s complaint over an unpaid ₹50,000 amount exposed a large illegal kidney racket in Kanpur, leading to the arrest of five doctors and raids on hospitals. The incident has prompted a wider investigation into organ trade links, raising concerns about healthcare regulations and enforcement. The case highlights potential gaps in monitoring illegal organ trafficking and medical malpractice in India.

articles.title: Crude oil prices reverse losses as Trump talks on Iran war; Brent above $105
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices rebounded above $105 per barrel after earlier declines, driven by discussions around potential U.S. military action in Iran. The price had dropped 2.7% on Wednesday before reversing losses. This volatility could impact India's energy costs and policy considerations, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: Brent crude prices reveresed losses and were above $105 after it had declined 1.8% to $99.37 a barrel post falling 2.7% on Wednesday.

articles.title: Will crude oil prices tank? Someone has bet nearly $1 billion on this via leverage
articles.summary: Investors have placed nearly $1 billion in leveraged bets on a potential decline in crude oil prices. The United States Oil Fund (USO) and United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw record inflows of $700 million and $600 million respectively in March, the highest since the pandemic. These movements reflect market sentiment and could impact India's energy costs and inflation, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: Its not just the bearish bets, even the bullish funds have drawn record money. The United States Oil Fund (USO) attracted flows worth nearly $700 million in March, the most since the pandemic, while the United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw flows worth a record $600 million.

This landmark sale, which more than doubled its estimate, highlights the enduring cultural significance of Varma's work. Poonawalla plans to make the national treasure available for public viewing.

Additionally, the endorsement of multiple new procurement strategies showcases a dynamic approach towards bolstering the nation’s defense arsenal.

articles.title: 'Buy from US or go take it from the Strait': Trump's suggestion to nations hit by oil shock due to Hormuz crisis
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump suggested countries facing fuel shortages due to the Middle East crisis could buy oil from the US or take stronger action to secure supplies. He claimed Iran's military capabilities have weakened. The crisis impacts global energy flows, raising concerns about supply disruptions and price volatility that could affect India's energy imports and policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: US President Donald Trump offered a solution to countries facing fuel shortages. He advised them to purchase oil from the United States. Alternatively, he suggested they take stronger actions to secure their own supplies. Trump stated Iran's military capabilities have been significantly weakened. This comes as the Middle East crisis continues, impacting global energy flows.

articles.title: 'Health Concerns Cannot Differ From Locality To Locality': Punjab & Haryana High Court Sets Aside Cancellation Of Mobile Tower Permission
articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has held that the health concerns of residents of a particular locality cannot be treated differently from those of the rest of the State, and cannot justify the selective cancellation of permission for the installation of a mobile tower. The Court observed that such reasoning is manifestly baseless when similar installations exist across the State in comparable localities. Justice Jagmohan Bansal was hearing a writ petition filed by Saral Mobile Project Service

articles.title: Despite revamp & AMC, Beijing embassy 'uninhabitable': CAG
articles.summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India has reported that residential units at the Chinese embassy in Beijing are uninhabitable, with many vacant or dilapidated. Officials are reportedly living in rented accommodations outside the embassy premises. The CAG's findings suggest systemic failures in the maintenance and management of diplomatic housing by the Indian government. This raises concerns about governance and administrative oversight in handling foreign missions' infrastructure.

articles.title: J&K govt announces revival of 120-yr-old power project
articles.summary: The Jammu and Kashmir government is reviving the 120-year-old Mohra Power Project, a 10.5MW hydroelectric facility dormant since the 1990s. The project, crucial for Jhelum River dredging, is being modernized as part of a broader plan to boost the region's energy capacity. The revival aims to restore vital infrastructure and enhance local power generation.

This century-old facility, crucial for Jhelum River dredging, is getting a modern upgrade. The project is a key part of a big plan to boost the region's energy output and bring this vital infrastructure back online.

The draft is open for public feedback before potential implementation.

articles.summary: India's financial obligations to the world decreased by $10.9 billion in the third fiscal quarter. articles.title: On global tensions and India’s economy
articles.summary: In an economy that imports more than four-fifths of its crude oil, external shocks transmitted through energy prices, shipping route, and volatile commodity markets can significantly reshape fiscal arithmetic. articles.title: Energy shocks come and go, India’s strength runs deeper
articles.summary: The key takeaway is not that India is insulated from global shocks, but that it is better equipped to manage them, with stress concentrated rather than systemic, buffers meaningful but finite, and policy flexibility available but requiring judicious use, writes Bhavdeep Bhatt, CEO, Northern Arc Investment Managers.

articles.summary: Brainbees Solutions’ subsidiary, Globalbees Brands, has raised its stake in Candes Technology from 62% to 92% by buying an additional 30% for Rs 37,250 per share. The deal was completed on March 31, 2026. Brainbees saw Q3 FY26 losses rise to Rs 38 crore amid higher expenses.

articles.title: AI videos, WhatsApp networks reshape campaign playbook in poll-bound states
articles.summary: Political parties in India are increasingly using AI for election campaigns, generating digital humans and personalized content to reach voters. While AI accelerates content production and targeted outreach, its direct electoral impact is still considered limited, with human intelligence and traditional methods remaining crucial for strategy.

articles.title: India has adequate seed stock available amid the Israel-Iran war, says govt official
articles.summary: India assures ample seed availability for the upcoming sowing season. This comes as global supply chains face disruptions. The nation boasts a self-reliant seed system. For the Kharif Season 2026, a surplus of 19.29 lakh quintals is reported. This ensures agricultural needs are met, providing stability for farmers.

articles.title: US tariffs drag India apparel export growth to modest 1.5%: ICRA
articles.summary: Indian apparel exports saw modest growth, impacted by US tariffs. A weaker rupee helped boost earnings in local currency. Shipments to the UK and UAE provided some relief. ICRA forecasts stronger revenue growth and improved financial health for exporters in the coming year. Geopolitical risks remain a concern for the sector.

articles.title: 10 foreign ships with energy cargo for India stranded in Persian Gulf
articles.summary: Ten foreign-flagged ships carrying energy cargo are stranded in the Persian Gulf, with 18 Indian vessels also in the region, raising concerns over Strait of Hormuz transit. The government prioritizes Indian ships, with two LPG carriers having successfully passed. Insurance costs have significantly increased due to the heightened risk.

articles.title: 'India's economy will double every 7-8 years,' says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat
articles.summary: India's economy is set for significant growth, aiming for 30 trillion dollars by 2047. However, ongoing global conflicts pose risks. These tensions could reduce India's economic expansion by one percent and increase inflation by 1.5 percent. The nation's reliance on imported energy makes it vulnerable to these disruptions. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: "Unfortunate for India," Ex R&AW Chief hints at probable inflation if West Asia situation escalates
articles.summary: Former R AW Chief Vikram Sood expressed concern over the West Asia conflict's impact on India, particularly potential oil and fertilizer shortages leading to inflation. He called the attack on Iran and the killing of its Supreme Leader unfortunate, emphasizing India's dependence on the Strait of Hormuz while noting Israel as an ally.

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war
articles.summary: India boosted diesel exports significantly in March. This surge was driven by favorable economics for middle distillates. Geopolitical events in West Asia tightened supply, increasing diesel and jet fuel prices. Refiners adjusted production to capitalize on these higher margins. India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. Officials anticipate an El Nino affecting monsoon rainfall. Production of tur and urad is expected to decline. This could lead to higher prices and food inflation.

articles.title: Licious’ FY26 revenues grow 47% to Rs 1,166 crore
articles.summary: Meat and seafood retailer Licious reported a 47% rise in revenues, at Rs 1,166 crore, for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, up from Rs 795 crore a year earlier. The company attributed its growth to strengthening its presence and improving delivery speed across key micro-markets in Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Delhi NCR. The company said it plans to expand to 10 micro-markets across these cities in FY27.

articles.title: Commercial LPG gets costlier, price hiked by  ₹195.5 amid global oil concerns
articles.summary: The price of a 19 kg commercial LPG cylinder will now be ₹2,078.50 in Delhi, news agencies reported, citing state oil companies.
articles.raw_summary: The price of a 19 kg commercial LPG cylinder will now be ₹2,078.50 in Delhi, news agencies reported, citing state oil companies.

articles.title: IndiGo ‘privileged’ to be led by new CEO Willie Walsh, co-founder Rahul Bhatia says
articles.summary: IndiGo couldn't have found a finer leader to grow the airline in India and the immediate neighbourhood, co-founder Rahul Bhatia says of new CEO William Walsh.

articles.title: India’s CCTV ban starts tomorrow: Will your home camera stop working?
articles.summary: Chinese CCTV camera manufacturers will face increased scrutiny in India starting April 1. However, these changes will not impact the devices already in use.
articles.raw_summary: Chinese CCTV camera manufacturers will face increased scrutiny in India starting April 1. However, these changes will not impact the devices already in use.

articles.title: ‘Come to draw strength’: Iran's Araghchi joins crowd at Tehran street as war goes on
articles.summary: A video, posted by Iran Consulate General in Mumbai, showed Araghchi attending a nighttime gathering on the eve of the Islamic Republic Day.

articles.title: Indian techie on H-1B accused of 'scam' by Texas whistleblower Sara Gonzales over food truck
articles.summary: A video of a heated argument between an Indian tech professional on H-1B and US-based whistleblower Sara Gonzales over a food truck in Texas has gone viral.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | West Asia subsea cable threats: What could happen to India’s internet amid Iran war?
articles.summary: Subsea cables carrying India's data face damage risk in a volatile region. While a total blackout is unlikely, rerouting traffic could lead to noticeable delays for users and businesses across the country.
